member.name: Sarah Pochin
member.synopsis: Sarah Pochin is the Reform UK MP for Runcorn and Helsby , and has been an MP continually since 1 May 2025.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Courts: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the impact of court backlogs on victims of crime.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of March 2026, the Crown Court backlog stood at over 80,000 cases, with 22,000 open for over a year. Behind each of those cases is someone awaiting justice – victims putting their lives on hold and witnesses waiting to share their testimony. Justice de...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Courts: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many court hearings were postponed in England and Wales in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have interpreted your question to be asking for information on how many magistrates’ and Crown Court trials were ineffective in England and Wales in each of the last five years. An ineffective trial is one that does not go ahead on the scheduled trial ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of access to finance for small and medium-sized enterprises.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The British Business Bank’s Small Business Finance Markets Report 2026 found that around half of SMEs used external finance in 2025. SME lending increased between 2024 and 2025 – reaching £68 billion in nominal terms – but remains below pre-pandemic level...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: Access
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many railway stations in England operate without step-free access.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department continues to improve accessibility through programmes such as the Access for All scheme, including the announcement in January of 31 new projects progressing. There are currently 488 stations with no step-free access. A further 392 stations...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Bus Services: Rural Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of bus service reductions on rural communities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the impact that reductions to local bus services can have and knows the importance of reliable bus services in enabling people to stay connected and access education, work and vital services, particularly in rural areas where bus...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Homelessness
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of homelessness across England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Homelessness is far too high, but the government’s National Plan to End Homelessness sets out the steps that we are taking to get back on track to ending homelessness. The government publishes quarterly data including the number of households in temporary...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Temporary Accommodation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, how many households are living in temporary accommodation in each region of England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Homelessness is far too high, but the government’s National Plan to End Homelessness sets out the steps that we are taking to get back on track to ending homelessness. The government publishes quarterly data including the number of households in temporary...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs: Speech and Language Disorders
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the availability of speech and language support for primary school pupils.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government consultation, special educational needs and disabilities reform: putting children and young people first, revealed the critical role that speech and language therapy plays in supporting children with speech, language and communication needs...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of pharmacy closures on access to healthcare services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Business Services Authority publishes official statistics which include the number of pharmacies opening and closing each financial year. This information is available at the following link:https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/statistical-collections/general...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Pharmacy: Closures
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many community pharmacies have closed in England in each of the last 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Business Services Authority publishes official statistics which include the number of pharmacies opening and closing each financial year. This information is available at the following link:https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/statistical-collections/general...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Crimes of Violence: Acids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many offences involving acid attacks were recorded in England and Wales in each of the last 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls under the remit of the UK Statistics Authority.A response to the Parliamentary Question of 26th June is attached.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Offenders: Deportation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of deportation arrangements for foreign national offenders.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Foreign nationals who commit crime should be in no doubt that the law will be enforced and, where appropriate, we will pursue their deportation.Since this Government came to power almost 10,000 foreign national offenders (FNOs) have been returned either v...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Accident and Emergency Departments: Standards

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of regional disparities in access to NHS dental appointments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Access to National Health Service dental services continues to vary across regions. The Government has committed to improving access to NHS dental services, particularly in areas with historically lower levels of provision.The percentages of adults and ch...

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many GP appointments were missed in each of the last 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Missed appointments, recorded as ‘did not attends’, are collected through General Practice Appointment Data. We do not carry data for 2016. Data for 2017 and 2018 is incomplete. The data is available at the following link:https://digital.nhs.uk/data-and-i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
